lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is on the phone, Chandler and Monica are sitting in the living room, and Ross is in the kitchen as Rachel enters from her bedroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Phoebe, are you still on hold? I was supposed to call my Dad back like two hours ago.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, he clipped on, he said call him as soon as you get a chance, he's at Flimby's.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's Flimby's?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, yeah, that's the word I use when I can't remember the real thing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Hang up! That's it! Come on!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No! Rachel, that's what they want me to do. My warranty expires tomorrow, if I don't get through, they're not gonna fix my crappy, broken phone for free! We cannot let them win! It's us versus them!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ye-e-es!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, listen I gotta double check for tickets tonight. Who-who got what?

lines.speaker: Chandler, Phoebe, and Rachel
lines.text: I had one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I need two. I'm bringing Pete. My boyfriend. I have a boyfriend now!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Two it is. Ross, how about you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, yeah, I ah, I also need two.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? Who's number two?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Who's number two? One of the more difficult games sewer workers play.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, no, it's-it's just this person.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Like a date type (looks at Rachel) person?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, kinda. It's this woman from work. I hope that won't be too weird. Will it, Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. No, not at all, not at all. I actually was gonna bring someone myself, so...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But you said one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I meant, me plus one!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (to Phoebe and Chandler) Did ah, you guys mean you plus one?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, I'll see you tonight.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, bye-bye!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye-bye!
Ross exits

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I need a date! (runs to her bedroom)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey, you guys are finally gonna get to meet Kate!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Chandler) And I ah, borrowed some of your cologne. I hope she likes it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, what are you doing?! It's never gonna happen, she's seeing somebody.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, and I don't have any cologne.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: The green bottle next to the shaving cream.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! Worm medicine for the duck.
In horror, Joey wipes his neck and smells it.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is getting ready to go to Joey's premiere and Phoebe is still on hold.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Here you go. You can wear this. (hands her a sweater)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (on phone) Ooh, I'm setting the phone down. (does so) But I'm still here! Just don't go anywhere I'm still here. (starts to put on the sweater) Don't-don't switch or anything, 'cause I'm, I'm right here. (She has pulled the sweater over her head, but her head is stuck in a sleeve.) Just one sec. One sec! One second!! (She is now frantically trying to get the sweater on, as Monica returns from the bathroom.) Wait! One second! Just...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Monica, I'm scared!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. Honey, that's-that's a sleeve. Okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And! We also have speaker phone. (She turns on the speaker phone.)

lines.speaker: Hold Voice
lines.text: Please, stay on the line. Your call is important to us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, wait, you gotta hang up 'cause we're gonna be late.
Phoebe starts to hang up the phone, but...

lines.speaker: Hold Voice
lines.text: Thank you for your patience, you're the next caller.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes!! Yes!! I'm the next caller! You were gonna have me hang up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, carrying the chick and duck) Hey! Can you take a duck and a chick to the theatre?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Of course not.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. I just wanted them to hear it from somebody else.
Joey's Premiere, Rachel is already there with her date, Tommy who's played by Ben Stiller who will be in There's Something About Mary and Meet The Parents, as Ross and his date, Cailin, arrive.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hey Rach!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How are you? (She goes to kiss him on the cheek, but stops because of the dates and pats him on the shoulder.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So it's looks like we're the first ones here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, looks that way. First ones here! Wooo!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yay!!
An awkward silence follows.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Tommy, this is Ross. Ross, Tommy.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. Oh, I'm sorry, this is Cailin.
They all try and shake hands at once. They end up criss-crossing they're arms to shake each other's hand, and Ross says...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And! Break!!
Awkward laughter follows.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, uhh, I think I'm going to run to the ladies room.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Cailin
lines.text: I'll join you.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: I'll get our seats.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (The girls leave.) (to Tommy) So uh, well, this-this is uh, this is awkward.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well y'know 'cause Rachel and I used to go out.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Oh, I didn't, I didn't know that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Well then this is awkward. So what do you uh...

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: I think we're here.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Yeah.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (sees two people sitting in their seats.) Uh, huh. Excuse me, I'm sorry, I-I think you may be in our seats.

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Umm, no, I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Can-can we take a look at your ticket?

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Sure. (Hands him the ticket.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at ticket) Yep! Yeah, see this says D-13, and uh...

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Oh, well I thought that ah...

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Oh, you thought, huh? Yeah, well that didn't really work out too well for you did it you idiot!! What are you?! A moron!! Huh?! It says D-13! Okay?! Look you're surrounded by even numbers!! Did that give you some clue?!

lines.speaker: Man
lines.text: Uh, the usher told us to come...

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Oh! Oh! The usher must be right! What, with all that training they go through! Get out!! (They start to leave.) Here! (He throws him back his ticket.) (to Ross, calmly) Hey man, you want the aisle?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I'm good. (He sits down, stunned.)
The Theatre, at the post premiere party. The gang is already there, except Phoebe. Joey runs in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (seeing Joey) There he is!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: There's our star!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So, so, what'd ya think?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Almost as good as that play with the two naked girls on the see-saw.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I-I wasn't in that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sees Kate) Oh-oh, hey-hey, Kate! Listen I want you to meet everybody. Everybody, this is Kate.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (stepping in) Excuse me. Excuse me. (to Kate) Sweetheart! (Kisses her.) Come! (They leave.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So that's the girl you like.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (drags Chandler over to buffet table) I'm telling you, this guy Rachel is with is crazy! Okay? He viscously screamed at total strangers! I think he's baaad news!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait a minute, wait a minute, you don't like the guy Rachel's dating? Well, that's odd.
[cut to Joey, Rachel, and Tommy.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey, Lauren. Uh, you guys this is, this is Kate's understudy, Lauren.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi!

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey. Gosh, you look soo familiar.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Oh, yeah! I-I ran into you in the hallway in your building. It was right after I slept with Joey. He dumped me the next day.
[cut to Monica, Pete, and Cailin]

lines.speaker: Cailin
lines.text: So. How'd you guys meet?

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.text: Well ah, the short version is, I ah pursued her for a couple of months, then I gave her a check for 20,000 dollars, and she was mine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, and in the long version, I dump him for telling people the short version.
[cut to Joey as Estelle, Joey's agent, approaches]

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: Joey, sweetheart, you were fabulous!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey you guys, this is my agent, Estelle.

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: How do you do. (to Rachel and Monica) Ooh, you two girls were outstanding! (to Joey) Did they have representation?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, they-they weren't in the play.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We're not actors.

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: Ooh, what a shame! Because with her face (points to Monica) and her chest (points to Rachel) I could really put something together.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Could I borrow it?
[cut to later]

lines.speaker: Cailin
lines.text: (to Ross) Hi! Remember me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi! Yeah! Tommy's in line for the bathroom and someone just cut in front of him, I think he's gonna snap. (He's watching very intently)

lines.speaker: Cailin
lines.text: Ross, I'm gonna go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Go? Why?

lines.speaker: Cailin
lines.text: I don't know. Could be because I don't feel like standing around all night waiting for some guy who may or may not scream.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But-but Cailin, he definitely will scream.

lines.speaker: Cailin
lines.text: Good bye Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, oh-ho bye.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (entering carrying a newspaper) Here we go people! (starts reading the review) Boxing Day! The Lucille Lortel Theatre, blah-la-la-la... Ah-ha! Joey Tribianni, gives an uneven performance, but Mr. Tribianni is not the worst thing in this production.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes!!! Ha-ha-ha!

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Kate Miller's awkward and mannered portrayal is laughable. (Kate walks away depressed.) Badda-badda-badda. Ah-ha! Here it is! The direction by Marshal Talmant is... (stops, reads it again, and throws down the paper in disgust) Thank you, boys and girls, you've ruined my life. Please, stuff your talentless faces with my Mother's crab cakes! (starts to leave) Excuse me!!
Joey steps in a picks up the paper, the gang all look at him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Anyone mind if I save this?
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Rachel are returning. Phoebe is still on hold.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, is the play over?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah. Where were you?

lines.speaker: Hold Voice
lines.text: Thank you for your patience, you're the next caller.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You were the next caller five hours ago. You must be going crazy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nah. I kept myself busy.
Both Rachel and Monica walk into their bedrooms, stop, and come back into the living room with confused looks on their faces.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, yeah. (to Monica) I put your stuff in her room, and her stuff in your room.
They both look at each other, nod their heads 'All right' and follow their stuff into their new rooms.
The Theatre, after the party. Joey is trying to comfort Kate.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Fabulous.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen, drama critics they're nothing but, but people who couldn't make it as actors. You know what you should do?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Become a drama critic!

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (entering, drunk) I am hurt! (to Joey and Kate) A plague on both your houses! (walks away)

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: By the way, he dumped me tonight after he read my review.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, classy.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yep! I sure know how to pick 'em, huh? Y'know I gave up a part on a soap for this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! Yeah I ah, I gave up a job too.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Really. What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, de-clawing cats. Hey, tell ya what. Let me walk you home. We'll stop by every news stand and burn every copy of their Times and the Post.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Why the Post?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you didn't see the Post?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: No. You?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No. Why?
Kate's Apartment, Joey and Kate are returning from a night out on the town.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: (drunk) So you really think those newspapers are just jealous of me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, absolutely! You're talented and you're good looking.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Oh, you're sweet and cute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! That's why they trashed me!
They kiss.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa. Wait a minute, wait a minute.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I, I don't get you. I mean first, you hate me. Then you sleep with me. Then you want nothing to do with me, now you want me again.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: What? So you never went out with an actress before?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Kate, do you even like me?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Of course I do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well so, how come you blew me off? Y'know? How come you were with him?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I don't know! I just, just do this! I-I always have to pick the like the smartest guy, or-or the most talented guy... Why can't I just pick someone like you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: You know what I mean. I mean like the sweetest guy. Joey, you're just so, you're so, so... (She passes out and slumps against his shoulder.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Checks to see if she's drooling on his shirt.) Okay. Okay, okay, hey. (Lays her down and covers her with a blanket.) There we go, let's get your feet up there. (Looks at her) Good night, Kate. Sweet dreams. (Picks up a garbage can) I'm gonna put this can right here in case you have to hurl.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, the next morning, Phoebe is STILL on hold.

lines.speaker: Hold Voice
lines.text: Please stay on the line. You're call is very important to us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from her bedroom) Pheebs, you've been up for 24 hours! Go to sleep, honey. Th-this isn't healthy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, I'm fine, and y'know why? 'Cause of all the riboflavin.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, happily) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! Didn't you have that outfit on last night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! I stayed at Kate's, but ah, nothing happened. Hey, Pheebs, where were ya?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so, so, so sorry, Joey. I definitely am gonna see you're play. I swear you're play is very important to us, thank you for your patience. You're play is the next play is the next play I'm gonna see.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Anyway, how did it go with Kate?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it was great! Yeah, I-I walked her home, and it was amazing how much we connected, y'know? Then ah, then she passed out, but then she woke up. Yeah? And we stayed up all night talking, and now we're like totally crazy about each other!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, you had the night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When two people finally realise their feelings for each other, and-and they talk for hours, and they-they learn all about the other person!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You-you think?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Did you like learn about her family?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Two brothers, one died!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! (They hug, triumphantly.)
Central Perk, Chandler and Monica are sitting on the couch, Rachel is on the chair.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: (bringing Chandler a cup) This is from the woman at the bar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho-ho-ho.
He turns and looks at her. The woman whispers something to Gunther; who comes back and takes the mug away from Chandler.

lines.speaker: Gunther
lines.text: Sorry. She thought you were somebody else.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What time is it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: One.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: 7:15. (Monica looks at him) Watch doesn't work.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Tommy's supposed to be here soon, we're going to lunch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look. Look, I wasn't going to say anything to you, but... All right, I don't think you should be seeing Tommy anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! The guy is mean. I mean really mean. I think you should stay away from him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, or, maybe, I should stay away from all men.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, it's not just 'cause I'm jealous. (Both Monica and Chandler give him a 'Come on' look) I mean I'm not, I'm not, I'm not jealous, okay? It's... Look, the guy, he screamed, he actually screamed at this couple sitting in our seats.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, and at the end of the play, he, he got up y'know, and he just started like, (claps his hands) banging his hands together!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, fine, fine. You don't want to believe me? No, that's fine. (starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're kidding!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross, don't. Ross!
Monica; Ross!
[cut to outside of Central Perk]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You don't want to believe me, I'm Mr. Funny to you. Mr. Funny... (turns around and almost spills his coffee on Tommy)

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Whoa!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa, sorry Tommy.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: What's in the cup, Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: What is in the cup?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, it's coffee.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Ice coffee? Tell me it's ice coffee!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's-it's hot...

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Hot coffee!!! You idiot!! You were gonna spill hot coffee all over me, huh?!! What are you just some big, dumb, stupid, doofy idiot, with a doofy idiot hairdo, huh?! Huh?!
[cut to inside Central Perk, we see Ross quietly tapping on the window, desperately trying to get the gang's attention, while Tommy is still screaming at him]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (not seeing Ross) What's your favourite thing about summertime?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, going to the beach. When it stays light real late.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah...

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: (entering, finished with yelling at Ross, who follows him in shell shocked) (happily) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Tommyyyy! Say, what's your favourite thing about summer?

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Ooh, I don't know. Probably the smell of freshly cut grass.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ohh, that's a good one.
Ross is having a fit, about how calm Tommy is now
Backstage at Joey's play, Joey is arriving, late.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sorry! Sorry, I'm late; sorry, I'm late! My duck and my chick and a fight, it-it was ugly.

lines.speaker: Stage Manager
lines.text: Look, we held the curtain for you buddy. Come on, let's go! Let's go!
[cut to onstage with Lauren standing in for Kate, the doorbell rings.]

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (answering the door) Vic! Where have you been, baby?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (surprised it's Lauren) (hugs her) (whispering) Where's Kate?

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (whispering) She got a job in L.A.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stunned) What?!

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: I've been waiting up all night for ya. Where have you been? (Joey doesn't answer) Where have you been? Vic?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, ahh, go to the window. I'm wanna run down to the truck and show you something.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (at the window, she's looking down out of the window) What do you got down there, Vic? What do you got under that tarp?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (grabs the window from the outside and sticks his head in) (whispering) When is she leaving?

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (whispering) Tonight. What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (making like he is yelling up to the second floor) I'm coming up!
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Ross are in the kitchen, Rachel and Monica are sitting in the living room, and Phoebe is, you guessed it, still on hold.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Hey, mind if I use the phone?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I... (starts jabbering incoherently)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why don't you use ours across the hall, 'cause she...has...problems.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (coming out of the kitchen with a cup of coffee, almost running into Tommy) Oh-ho, whoa! Sorry, Tommy. I almost spilled this hot coffee on you.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Yeah, but you didn't.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, but it's-it's-it's hot!
We see Tommy go into Joey and Chandler's apartment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, would you just stop it! It's getting really old.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe no one believes me!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I do, I believe you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You do Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. But I also believed her (points to the phone) when she said I was next.
[cut to Chandler and Joey's apartment, Tommy is using the phone]

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: (noticing the chick) Ooh, hey! Hey, there little fella. (picks up the chick) Mr. Fuzzy-Man, how are you doing? (starts to pet him) Aww. (The chick poops on his hand.) Eww! Oh! Eww! Gross! Idiot!! Stupid little, fuzzy, yellow creature!! Oh look at me, I'm so cute, I'm a little chick who's disgusting! God, you're so stupid, how are you not yet extinct!! (the duck wattles behind him and quacks) (to the duck) Quack-quack, quack-quack!! What are you quacking about?! Dumb Donald Doo-Doo!!
We see the gang staring at him through the doorways.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Step away from the duck.

lines.speaker: Tommy
lines.text: Ooh, sorry little Mr. Chic-A-Dee, sorry you went doody in my hand! (starts to walk out and stops) (to Rachel) Well, I guess we're not going out anymore. Whaa!!!
He leaves the gang in stunned silence.
Backstage at Joey's play, Joey is changing for the next scene as Kate arrives, carrying her bags.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I'm soo glad I caught you, I couldn't find you before.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wh-wh-what's going on? Wh-what's this about L.A.?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: They still want me for General Hospital.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But, but wh-what about us?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Last night was wonderful. But I-I can't stay here just for you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, so, stay for the museums!

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Stage Manager
lines.text: Joey, onstage!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well can you at least stay to the end of the play? I mean, I'll go to the airport with ya, I-I wanna say good bye.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (yelling from onstage) Where are you Vic?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Flight's in an hour. I-I gotta go.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Vic! Vic! Vic!!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Lauren) In a minute!!
[cut to the end of the play]

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: So this is it? Victor?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I guess it is. (sees Kate is watching, he turns Lauren so that he can talk directly to Kate, but still look like he is talking to Lauren) Listen, I ah, I gotta say good bye, and-and I gotta say it quick 'cause this is killing me. One thing you gotta know, is that I will never forget you. But, you've got things you have to do now, and so do I. And so... I'm gonna get on this spaceship, (smoke starts pouring in from the ceiling, and a ladder comes down, with flashing, colored lights on the side of it) and I'm gonna go to Blargon 7 in search of alternative fuels. But when I return, 200 years from now, you'll be long gone. But I won't have aged at all. (gets on the ladder) So you tell your great-great-granddaughter to look me up, because Adrienne, baby, I'm gonna want to meet her.
The ladder retracts, taking Joey up into the spaceship for his voyage to Blargon 7, and Kate waves good bye.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: So long, Vic! (waving good bye as the ladder retracts)
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, the gang, except Joey, is there. Phoebe is, well you y'know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, it's been two days.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I know. Oh, good thing it's one of those 801 numbers. Right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, 800 is toll free, 801 is-is Utah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, no, oh no-no-no, it's has to be 800. (picks up the instruction manual to check the phone number) 'Cause all those big companies have 800 numbers, every one. (Finds the number) Yeah, every big Utah-based company has one.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoe-be!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sorry, I'm so sorry, I will pay you back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And yet, she's still not hanging up the phone.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hang it up! Hang up the phone!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine! Fine! (slams the phone down, breaking it) Oh-oh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I think I broke it. But that's all right, here's the number you can call.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh.
End
